explanation: Unpredictable is a natural adjective for discussing weather and changing conditions in social conversation.
usage:
Pattern: the weather is ...
  • The weather is amazing today.
  • The weather has been awful all week.
spanish: El clima ha sido tan impredecible últimamente.

scenario: Everyday English: What Did You Do Yesterday? 🗓️
text: I just {{c1::hung out}} with some friends yesterday.
explanation: To hang out means to spend time casually with people, often in a relaxed or informal way.
usage:
Pattern: hang out with
  • I hung out at the park.
  • We hung out after work.
spanish: Ayer simplemente pasé el rato con unos amigos.

explanation: To be into something means to like it or be interested in it a lot.
usage:
Pattern: I’m into ...
  • I’m into cooking.
  • She’s really into music.
spanish: Me interesa mucho la fotografía y el senderismo.

text: I try not to talk too much about {{c1::politics}} at work.
explanation: Politics is an everyday topic, but people often use it carefully in social or professional contexts.
usage:
Pattern: talk about politics / political issues
  • Politics is a sensitive topic.
  • We discussed political issues at dinner.
spanish: Intento no hablar demasiado de política en el trabajo.

explanation: Pretty chill means relaxed, low-stress, and not too busy. This is very natural in conversational English.
usage:
Pattern: pretty chill / keep it simple
  • We’re keeping it pretty chill.
  • I just want a chill weekend.
spanish: Vamos a tener un fin de semana bastante relajado.

text: I caught the {{c1::news}} this morning before work.
explanation: To catch the news means to watch or hear the news briefly. It is a natural phrase in daily conversation.
usage:
Pattern: catch the news / hear the news
  • I caught the news on the radio.
  • Did you hear the news?
spanish: Vi las noticias esta mañana antes de ir al trabajo.

text: It’s been a pretty {{c1::chill}} week so far.
explanation: Chill is a casual adjective meaning relaxed or calm. It is used a lot in informal speech.
usage:
Pattern: pretty chill / a chill day
  • It’s a chill evening.
  • We had a chill lunch together.
spanish: Ha sido una semana bastante tranquila hasta ahora.

text: I’m not really sure {{c1::what to think}} about it.
explanation: This phrase is very common in casual conversation when someone is uncertain or ambivalent.
usage:
Pattern: not sure what to think
  • I’m not sure what to think about the new policy.
  • She wasn’t sure what to say.
spanish: No estoy realmente seguro de qué pensar al respecto.
